name: Willie Cook
abstract: Willie Cook (November 11, 1923 – September 22, 2000) was an American jazz trumpeter. Cook was born in Tangipahoa, Louisiana, on November 11, 1923.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: When they pass out a piece of music, you saw the names: ... Willie Cook, ... Clark Terry ... All the people’s names were on the music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: the trumpet section was Elmon Wright... and Willie Cook... And myself, that was the trumpet section
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: featuring ... Willie Cook ... Juan Tizol ... Duke Ellington and his orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Happy Caldwell) does not mention Willie Cook. The interviewer mentions Willie Cook in the context of a misattribution of solos on Earl Hines records, which the interviewee does not address or confirm personal knowledge of.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...about your being with Ellington one day, and Paul Gonsalves and Ray Nance were in jail in Las Vegas, or something like that. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah, Willie Cook.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: Willie Cook was playing the lead trumpet, and they had a lot of good musicians, and... I just remember hearing that band, and taking a liking to it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
